title: Incorrect nested mapping slot derivation — wrong key order or wrong hash input size
wrong_code:
// WRONG A — inverted key order and wrong input region
mstore(0x0c, caller())
mstore(0x00, from)
let slot1 := keccak256(0x0c, 0x20)  // only hashes 32 bytes from 0x0c
mstore(0x0c, slot1)
mstore(0x00, 0x01)
let allowanceKey := keccak256(0x0c, 0x20)

// WRONG B — uses scratch space offset (0x0c) but hashes wrong region
mstore(0x0c, _ALLOWANCE_SEED)
mstore(0x00, from)
let outer := keccak256(0x0c, 0x20)  // misses the seed overlap trick
explanation: Solidity derives nested mapping slots as: slot(map[k1][k2]) = keccak256(k2 . keccak256(k1 . declared_slot)). The outer key (k1) and the mapping's declared slot number are hashed first to get the intermediate slot, then the inner key (k2) is hashed with that to get the final slot. Both hash inputs must be exactly 64 bytes (key padded to 32 + slot padded to 32). Using keccak256(0x0c, 0x20) only hashes 32 bytes which produces a completely wrong slot.
why_wrong: Wrong slot derivation causes sload to read from a completely different storage location than where Solidity wrote the data. The contract silently reads zero or stale data instead of the actual allowance, making every transferFrom either always pass (reads 0 < amount = false check passes) or always fail with wrong values.
gas_impact: Zero gas difference — this is a critical correctness bug that silently corrupts state
